justification: A correctly excludes GraphQL (candidate only ran read-only queries written by senior devs — no real authorship or demonstrated skill), VS Code and Chrome DevTools (IDE/tooling environment, not technical skills), and produces a clean 4-skill list matching exactly what the expected behavior demands. B incorrectly includes GraphQL despite the resume making clear the candidate did not write queries, and pads the list with VS Code and Chrome DevTools. A also extracts and classifies both projects correctly as 'Frontend Only' with appropriate complexity ratings and technology lists, while B extracts no projects at all (neutral score of 3). Both outputs score equally on impact_quality since neither has real numeric metrics to work with — A's impact highlight is vague ('3-month internship' is a duration, not an impact metric), but B provides nothing to compare. A wins decisively on skill_accuracy and conservatism, and edges out B on project_classification by actually providing correct classifications.
